character_dossiers.name: Long John Silver
character_dossiers.biography: A one-legged tavern keeper with a crutch, described as tall, strong, and cheerful. Known for his interactions with customers and the narrator.

character_dossiers.name: Black Dog
character_dossiers.biography: A buccaneer mentioned in dialogue, recognized by the narrator as a former customer at the Admiral Benbow.

character_dossiers.name: the tallow-faced man
character_dossiers.biography: A man with a tallow face missing two fingers, previously seen at the Admiral Benbow.

character_dossiers.name: Tom Morgan
character_dossiers.biography: An old, grey-haired sailor with a mahogany face, described as rolling a quid and speaking with a crew member.

character_dossiers.name: Ben
character_dossiers.biography: A runner associated with the tavern, tasked with chasing Black Dog.

character_dossiers.name: the squire
character_dossiers.biography: A character who gives a note to the narrator addressed to Long John Silver.

character_dossiers.name: the blind man
character_dossiers.biography: A blind beggar previously seen at the Admiral Benbow, recognized by the narrator.

character_dossiers.name: Pew
character_dossiers.biography: A blind beggar previously seen at the Admiral Benbow, recognized by the narrator.

character_dossiers.name: Harry
character_dossiers.biography: A character who recognizes Black Dog and alerts Long John Silver.

character_dossiers.name: the captain
character_dossiers.biography: A character mentioned in dialogue, referenced by Long John Silver as part of a buccaneer group.
